rows.finalText: For "What is the next right step in my calling", the cards keep circling back to purpose / calling themes with a right path emphasis. Rider (1) in Past sets the tone with expect updates that change your short-term plan. Present turns on Stork (17), bringing change is active and potentially beneficial to the hinge of the reading and asking for one careful adjustment. Here, Stork emphasizes improvement, transition, upgrades, and the feeling that something is trying to move into a better form. The likely direction shows in Future through Clover (2), and it suggests small improvements compound if acted on quickly once the pivot is met directly. Clover often points to small improvements compound if acted on quickly, especially where luck, window, and ease have been building quietly in your calling. The path becomes clearest where Rider (1) meets Stork (17): a blend of news and change, asking you to respond promptly and confirm facts while staying alert to constant switching without consolidation. Stork (17) is the real hinge in this spread. Stork colors your calling through improvement, transition, upgrades, and the feeling that something is trying to move into a better form. Rider (1) with Stork (17) keep emphasizing a blend of news and change, asking you to respond promptly and confirm facts while staying alert to constant switching without consolidation. Sequence improvements deliberately so Clover (2) can land as lived experience rather than just possibility.
